reasoning: The market was at 7% on October 30, 2025. This extremely low price strongly implies that the 2-hour barrier was not broken during the major fall marathons (Berlin and Chicago). With only the Valencia Marathon remaining in December as a realistically fast course before the end of 2025, and with Kelvin Kiptum (the world record holder) having passed away and Eliud Kipchoge past his prime, there are no active runners realistically poised to break the 2-hour legal barrier in late 2025. The 7% market price likely represents a long-shot bias. Therefore, a 5% estimate represents the best evaluation given the extremely narrow remaining window for 2025.
